data.title: ProShares' Money Market ETF Sees $17 Billion Trading Volume on Launch Day
data.description: ProShares has introduced its money market ETF, IQMM, specifically targeting U.S. stablecoin issuers. According to NS3.AI, this ETF achieved a remarkable $17 billion in trading volume on its first day. This marks the first financial product to align with the U.S. stablecoin regulation act, GENIUS, which has been in effect since July of last year. In comparison, BlackRock's similar stablecoin money market ETF, IBIT, saw $1 billion in trading volume on its launch day in January 2024.

data.title: Trump announces 10% global tariff following SCOTUS ruling
data.description:
The United States Supreme Court ruled on Friday that President Donald Trump could not use national emergency powers to levy tariffs during peacetime.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/trump-10-global-tariff-scotus-ruling?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Polymarket faces Dutch shutdown order as KSA cites gambling
data.description:
Polymarket ban Netherlands: KSA orders the site to cease for offering unlicensed games of chance under Dutch law, with potential €840k fines and EU scrutiny.
source: https://coincu.com/news/polymarket-faces-dutch-shutdown-order-as-ksa-cites-gambling/

data.title: Bitcoin Core Integrates Cluster Mempool for Enhanced Transaction Management
data.description: Bitcoin Core has integrated a new transaction mempool system known as Cluster Mempool. According to NS3.AI, this redesigned system organizes transactions into clusters and chunks, aligning mempool operations more closely with mining incentives. The upgrade aims to simplify fee estimation, enhance the security of second layer protocols, and optimize block construction by addressing conflicts from previous methods. The new architecture is designed to improve transaction replacement reliability, reduce eviction unpredictability, and support Bitcoin's decentralized system.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. Supreme Court Decision on IEEPA Tariffs Raises Concerns for Mexico
data.description: Banco Base analyst Gabriela Siller has expressed concerns following the U.S. Supreme Court's decision to overturn U.S. President Donald Trump's IEEPA tariffs ruling. According to Jin10, Siller noted that this development might not be beneficial for Mexico, as goods entering under the USMCA agreement were previously exempt, but the current situation remains unclear. Trump has stated that tariffs under Section 232 and Section 301 will remain unchanged, and he plans to impose a 10% global tariff under Section 122, in addition to existing tariffs. Siller highlighted that this could lead to increased tariffs on Mexican automobiles, rising to 35%, and steel and aluminum tariffs reaching 60%. She also mentioned that the review of the USMCA agreement might become more challenging.

data.title: Opening the door: SEC issues guidance on brokers’ capital stablecoin requirements
data.description:
The SEC introduced new guidance allowing broker-dealers to apply a “2% haircut” to proprietary positions in certain stablecoins.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/390675/opening-the-door-sec-issues-guidance-on-brokers-capital-stablecoin-requirements?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Comments on Tariff Impact
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has indicated that the integration of tariff sections 122, 232, and 301 is expected to have minimal effect on overall tariff revenue. According to NS3.AI, this assessment is derived from estimates provided by the Treasury Department. The announcement underscores a delicate equilibrium in trade policy, suggesting that government income from tariffs will remain largely unaffected.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Plans Transformative Upgrades for Ethereum's Future
data.description: Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin has announced plans to implement transformative upgrades to ensure the network remains competitive amidst the rise of new blockchains. According to NS3.AI, Buterin aims to rebuild Ethereum internally over the next five years, focusing on advancements that will enhance its functionality and appeal. He emphasized the potential role of AI-driven coding and verification in accelerating the upgrade process, which could significantly impact the timeline and efficiency of these developments.

data.title: Leadership Change at Sanofi SA Following L’Oréal SA's Influence
data.description: Sanofi SA has undergone a significant leadership change with the departure of Chief Executive Officer Paul Hudson. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the involvement of L’Oréal SA in this corporate shakeup. The French pharmaceutical company has appointed a successor, marking a pivotal moment in its executive management. This development underscores the influence of L’Oréal SA in the decision-making process at Sanofi SA.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Canadian Businesses Face Uncertainty After U.S. Supreme Court Decision on Tariffs
data.description: Canadian businesses are advised to remain cautious following the U.S. Supreme Court's decision to overturn U.S. President Donald Trump's emergency tariffs. Bloomberg posted on X, experts suggest that this ruling ushers in a new, unpredictable phase of Trump's America First trade policy. The decision, while significant, does not eliminate the potential for further trade volatility, leaving Canadian companies to navigate an uncertain economic landscape.

data.title: AI Tools Used in Global Firewall Breaches
data.description: In the past five weeks, hackers have successfully infiltrated over 600 firewalls across multiple countries using readily accessible artificial intelligence tools. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the findings from security research conducted by Inc. The breaches underscore the growing threat posed by AI in cyberattacks, as these tools become more sophisticated and accessible to malicious actors. The incidents have raised concerns among cybersecurity experts about the potential for increased frequency and scale of such attacks in the future. Authorities and organizations are urged to enhance their security measures to combat these evolving threats.

data.title: Pentagon Allocates $80 Million for Idaho Mine Amid Speculation
data.description: The Pentagon has allocated $80 million to the owners of a mine in Idaho for the extraction of a mineral deemed critical for the U.S. military. Bloomberg posted on X, raising questions about whether there might be additional valuable resources beneath the surface. The funding underscores the strategic importance of securing domestic sources of essential minerals for national defense purposes. However, the potential for other undisclosed resources has sparked curiosity and speculation about the true value of the site. The situation highlights the ongoing efforts to bolster the United States' mineral supply chain amid global uncertainties.

data.title: SCOTUS strikes down Trump tariffs, but 'alternative' plans brewing
data.description:
US President Donald Trump has repeatedly said that tariffs could help pay down the $38 trillion, and growing, US national debt.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/scotus-strikes-trump-tariffs-alternative-plan?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: ConocoPhillips Considers Selling Permian Basin Assets
data.description: ConocoPhillips is reportedly considering the sale of certain assets in the Permian Basin as part of a strategic effort to streamline its portfolio. Bloomberg posted on X, citing sources familiar with the situation, that the company is evaluating its options regarding these assets. The move aligns with ConocoPhillips' broader strategy to optimize its operations and focus on core areas. The Permian Basin, located in West Texas and southeastern New Mexico, is one of the most prolific oil-producing regions in the United States. The potential sale reflects the company's ongoing assessment of its asset portfolio to enhance efficiency and shareholder value. Further details on the potential sale and its impact on ConocoPhillips' operations have not been disclosed.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Criticizes Supreme Court Justices Over Recent Ruling
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed dissatisfaction with the Supreme Court's recent decision against him, attributing the outcome to what he perceives as the political bias of the justices. According to Jin10, Trump stated that he believed the case was unwinnable, but political motivations and the desire to align with certain groups in Washington, D.C., influenced the justices' decision. When asked if the justices would still be invited to his upcoming State of the Union address, Trump confirmed the invitation remains, though he expressed indifference to their attendance. He was particularly critical of Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Neil Gorsuch, describing their involvement as a disgrace to their families, though he did not comment on whether he regretted appointing them.

data.title: Guaranteed Income Initiative Expands to Support Low-Income Mothers in Oregon
data.description: The Bridge Project is extending its guaranteed income program to Multnomah County, Oregon, with a budget of $2.025 million aimed at aiding 100 low-income mothers. According to NS3.AI, the initiative offers phased cash payments totaling $20,250 per participant over a span of approximately three years, with the goal of alleviating childhood poverty. To qualify, applicants must reside in Multnomah County, be pregnant (up to 27 weeks), be at least 18 years old, and have an annual income below $39,000. Payments are set to begin on March 1st.

data.title: William E. Ford to Attend 2026 Qatar Economic Forum
data.description: William E. Ford, Chairman and CEO of General Atlantic, is set to participate in the 2026 Qatar Economic Forum. Bloomberg posted on X that Ford, a prominent figure in the financial sector, will join other leaders at the event. The forum is expected to address key economic issues and foster discussions among global business leaders.

data.title: Crypto exchanges will need Russian office to avoid ban by Moscow
data.description:
Cryptocurrency exchanges will have to set up a subsidiary in Russia and abide by its laws to be allowed to work there, the country’s monetary authority has made that clear. The statement comes amid fears that Russian authorities are planning to cut off access to global trading platforms for digital assets as soon as they […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/crypto-exchanges-will-need-russian-office-to-avoid-ban/

data.title: Inside DeFi 005: ️ Base shuns Optimism, Discord’s KYC disaster
data.description:
Welcome back to Inside DeFi Coinbase-founded Base has decided to shun optimism, DeFi platforms are shutting down, and Discord has caused a new crypto controversy around its use of age verification technology. Elsewhere, Artificial Intelligence (AI) benchmarks suggest that AI is more useful to exploit protocols than patch them. Optimism’s glass is looking (more than) half empty Coinbase’s Base network announced this week that it would be pursuing a “new, unified stack,” leaving Optimism’s OP Stack infrastructure and moving to its own Base Chain. Faster shipping, a simpler architecture, and Ethereum alignment are the three objectives Base claims for the decision
source: https://protos.com/inside-defi-005-%f0%9f%8c%aa%ef%b8%8f-base-shuns-optimism-discords-kyc-disaster/
